narration: Most Americans think carrying a credit card balance helps their score. That is completely false — and believing it could cost you $92,000.
visual: Credit card SVG with $6,194 balance slides up from below. 'CARRYING THIS BUILDS CREDIT?' text fades in, then a bold red MYTH badge springs onto screen.

narration: Fifty-six percent of Americans believe a balance boosts their credit. Banks know this myth exists — and they're definitely not rushing to correct you.
visual: Large '56%' number counts up from zero on a light background. A red progress bar fills to 56%. Below: 'OF AMERICANS BELIEVE A BALANCE BUILDS CREDIT FASTER' and 'THAT IS 100% FALSE' reveals.

narration: Here's how FICO actually works. It tracks your utilization — how much of your limit you're using. Carrying a balance raises that number, which hurts your score.
visual: Five FICO score factor bars animate in: Payment History 35%, Amounts Owed 30%, etc. The 'AMOUNTS OWED' bar pulses red to show how a balance makes this factor worse.

narration: The average American carries $6,194 on their card at 22% interest. That's $1,362 in pure interest every single year — paid for a belief that is provably wrong.
visual: Balance counter animates from $0 to $6,194. Then '× 22% APR' appears, then '= $1,362 / YEAR IN WASTED INTEREST' springs in red. A green dollar bill SVG sits below.

narration: Invest that $1,362 a year instead at 5% for 30 years. You'd be sitting on $92,000. You've been trading a small fortune for a myth — and that's wild.
visual: Two bar columns grow side by side: 'INTEREST PAID' reaches $40,860 while 'INVESTED INSTEAD' towers at $92,000. Labels appear when bars finish growing.

narration: Pay your card in full every single month. Your score improves, interest disappears, and you keep the $92,000. Drop 'MYTH' in the comments if this changed how you see your card.
visual: Green-themed credit card SVG labeled 'PAID IN FULL' springs in with a large green checkmark. 'PAY IN FULL EVERY MONTH' and 'KEEP YOUR $92,000' text fades in. Red CTA badge appears at bottom.
